summary: The International Classification of Diseases-10 (ICD-10) is an internationally recognized system used primarily for classifying diagnoses across medical fields including psychiatry. It serves as the standardized framework developed by WHO for identifying and categorizing various disorders such as those related to mood, behavior, and neurological issues like post-concussion syndrome.
